mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which compounds into sustained corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series signals, or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of co-occurring expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production levels reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and higher S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with industrial production changes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Market absorbs inflation surprises without negative returns, or the threshold fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in over 55% of observations or shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth rates reflect stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly improves corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently decouple from equity performance, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production below its 6-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing demand and impending earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO drops below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances or the moving average crossover fails to generate a statistically significant predictive edge.
